faqs.a: Cyclothymia is a long-lasting mood condition with many shifts between mild highs and mild lows that don't reach the full intensity of mania or major depression. The diagnosis involves these ups and downs continuing over a long stretch, usually at least two years in adults.

faqs.q: How is cyclothymia different from bipolar disorder?
faqs.a: Cyclothymia sits on the bipolar spectrum as a milder, chronic form, where the highs and lows don't reach the level of mania or major depression. Over time, some people with cyclothymia go on to develop fuller bipolar episodes.

No. The shifts in cyclothymia are persistent and last over years, and they cause real disruption to relationships, work, and self-image. It's a recognized condition that can be understood and managed with the right support.
